disclaimer: This guide is for informational purposes only. Visa policies and entry requirements change frequently. Always verify current requirements with the official Chinese Embassy or Consulate in Spain before making travel arrangements. This is not legal advice.
visaFreeNote: Spain is part of the EU Schengen area. China has periodically offered visa-free transit arrangements (72/144-hour) for certain nationalities at specific ports. Check current policy as rules change frequently.

applicationSteps.title: Determine visa type
applicationSteps.detail: Identify the correct visa category for your trip purpose (tourism L, business M, work Z, student X, transit G). Each has different requirements.

applicationSteps.title: Gather required documents
applicationSteps.detail: Prepare all required documentation including passport, photos, application form, and supporting materials specific to your visa type.

applicationSteps.title: Complete application form
applicationSteps.detail: Fill out the Chinese Visa Application Form (Form V.2013) online or on paper. Ensure all information matches your passport exactly.

applicationSteps.title: Submit application
applicationSteps.detail: Apply in person at the Chinese Embassy in Madrid, Consulate in Barcelona, or through the Chinese Visa Application Service Center (CVASC). Appointment may be required.

applicationSteps.title: Collect visa
applicationSteps.detail: Pick up your passport with the visa stamp on the specified date. Verify all details are correct before leaving.

requiredDocuments: Valid Spanish passport with at least 6 months validity and 2 blank pages
Recent passport-size color photo (48mm x 33mm, white background)
Flight itinerary showing round-trip entry and exit dates
Hotel reservation or invitation letter from host in China
Proof of financial means (bank statements from last 3 months)

processingTime.rush: 1 business day (additional fee, subject to approval)

fees.note: Fees vary by visa type and processing speed. Spanish citizens may pay different rates than some other EU nationals.
fees.serviceCenter: CVASC charges an additional service fee

embassyInfo.embassy: Embassy of the People's Republic of China in Madrid
embassyInfo.serviceCenter: Chinese Visa Application Service Center (CVASC) locations in Madrid and Barcelona

entryPorts: Most international airports with customs facilities
Major land border crossings (subject to change)

customsRules.limit: Amounts exceeding 20,000 CNY or $5,000 USD equivalent must be declared upon entry and exit
Personal electronics for tourist use generally allowed; items for commercial use may require declaration
Bring prescription medications with doctor's note; some substances are prohibited in China
Tobacco: 400 cigarettes, Alcohol: 1.5 liters. Gifts up to 5,000 CNY value duty-free

arrivalTips: Fill out the Arrival/Departure Card (usually distributed on your flight) before landing
Have your hotel address written in Chinese characters to show taxi drivers or immigration
Keep your passport accessible as you will need it at immigration and at many hotels
Consider downloading a VPN before arrival if you need access to services blocked in mainland China
WeChat and Alipay are widely used; set up mobile payment before or upon arrival if possible

healthRequirements.note: Health requirements may change. Check current entry health requirements before departure.
healthRequirements.insurance: Travel health insurance covering China is strongly recommended

usefulLinks.title: Spanish Ministry of Foreign Affairs Travel Advisories
